details: hardshrink SFPU kernel files have been nuked from this repo. Found in enum but not in unary_op_utils.cpp switch statements. Will search for surviving SFPU kernel code and reference files.

ckernel_sfpu_swish.h found in both wormhole_b0 and blackhole with identical implementations. Uses SFPI abstractions (vFloat, dst_reg, v_if). Piecewise sigmoid approximation: polynomial for |x|<=2.5, linear for 2.5<|x|<=5, saturation for |x|>5.

hardshrink uses TWO custom compute kernels: hardshrink_kernel.cpp (binary_dest_reuse FPU path) and hardshrink_kernel_sfpu.cpp (copy_tile + add/mul SFPU path). Both use ltz_tile/gtz_tile/fill_tile SFPU operations. The get_compute_kernel_path case was nuked but the kernel files survive.

All SFPU identifiers verified: calculate_swish (WH+BH), llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_swish, llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_swish_init. All file paths verified. SFPI abstractions confirmed: abs->SFPABS, vConst1->Fixed Const 2 (1.0), v_if->CC push/pop. Ready to write analysis.

HARDSHRINK uses a custom compute kernel (hardshrink_kernel_sfpu.cpp or hardshrink_kernel.cpp), NOT eltwise_sfpu.cpp with SFPU_OP_CHAIN_0. It composes multiple FPU (add_binary_tile, sub_binary_tile, mul_binary_tile) and SFPU (fill_tile, ltz_tile, gtz_tile) operations in a two-pass algorithm with a tmp circular buffer.

key_findings: hardshrink uses custom compute kernel with multi-step algorithm: fill_tile(lambda), copy input, add/sub, ltz/gtz comparison, mul for masking, two-pass with tmp CB. SFPU ops used: _calculate_fill_, _calculate_comp_ (less_than_zero, greater_than_zero), _calculate_sfpu_binary_ (ADD, SUB, MUL)

cc_pattern: v_if/v_else/v_endif for less-than-zero and greater-than-zero comparisons

details: swish_analysis.md written to .claude-analysis/softcap-1/. Covers: piecewise sigmoid approximation (3 segments), SFPI-based kernel (Style A), 9 SFPU instructions documented, address mode ADDR_MOD_7, WH/BH identical implementations.
